question.text: The Extreme Loss Margin (ELM) in spread trading applies:

answer.text: Individually to both legs of the spread position
Only to the net spread position
Not at all — spreads are exempt from ELM

explanation.text: **The Logic:** While initial margin gets a spread benefit (up to 75%), ELM is applicable individually to both legs of the positions. There's no ELM reduction for spreads. **The Trap:** ELM is NOT reduced by spreading. It applies to each leg independently. Spreads reduce initial margin but not ELM. **Cross-Exam Context:** Margin framework is specific to commodity derivative regulation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Factor both initial margin (with spread benefit) AND ELM (no benefit) into capital planning for spread trades — total margin is lower than outright but not negligible.
